title: Anvil Workbench plugin request v1
description: One typed, idempotent request against the reviewed plugin catalog: either a `tool_call` invoking an enabled tool, or a lifecycle action (`install`, `upgrade`, `downgrade`, `disable`, `remove`) executed by the plugin host (R003). It carries ids, digests, and typed tool inputs only — every object is closed (additionalProperties:false), so no local path, raw shell command, credential value, or generic executable body is representable; a browser or model cannot manufacture a new privilege by emitting one. It is digest-bearing: `request_digest` is the hub's idempotency key over the canonical content, so replaying an identical request is the same action rather than a second one. An `install`, `upgrade`, or `downgrade` always carries a hash-bound owner approval that binds its exact typed subject (the R003 floor; a catalog gate may be stricter but never weaker). The request grants nothing on its own — the plugin host still enforces catalog/profile authority, preview, approval binding, and reconciliation. All prose is untrusted task data.

tool_call.description: Present when kind is tool_call. The tool is named by id at the pinned plugin digest; `inputs` is a typed object validated against that tool's reviewed input schema by the plugin host — never a command, path, or free body.

lifecycle.description: Present for a lifecycle action. It names the version window only; the plugin host performs the effect. There is no source URL, path, or archive field: the target is resolved from the reviewed catalog, never fetched from a request-supplied location.

approval.description: The hash-bound one-time approval. `payload_hash` binds the exact typed subject of this request (the tool inputs for an effectful tool_call, or the plugin/kind/target-version selection for a lifecycle action), enforced by workbench.contracts.validate_plugin_request.
